macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). Wednesday's session saw a sharp reversal in risk appetite after Iran attacked commercial tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, collapsing the ceasefire. WTI surged 3.2% to $74.57 and oil-sensitive equities plunged, while the Fed's June minutes revealed a hawkish split with nine members projecting rate hikes before year-end. The SOXX rallied 3.2%, led by memory and Apple-related chip names, even as broader market gains were capped by renewed energy inflation fears. The June NY Fed survey showed inflation expectations rising, and the US trade deficit widened to $77.6B as businesses front-ran tariffs.
commodities_note: WTI jumped to $74.57 as shipping through the Strait stopped. Gold slid 1.4% to $4,077 as yields rose off hawkish minutes. The US10Y pushed 4.57%, tightening financial conditions even as the VIX remained relatively subdued at 16.9.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain overbought with RSI across 79–86, no fresh catalysts. NIM compression from elevated US rates and MAS S$NEER appreciation still the structural headwind — wait for pullbacks to respective supports.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally challenged by dual NIM compression; tactically overbought
singapore.key_development: No new developments; extension continues across the board.

semiconductors.summary: Semis staged a sharp bounce (SOXX +3.2%) led by memory and Apple deals, but the sector is still down 12% over the week. MU and AVGO gained on specific catalysts, while TSMC and ASML face near-term earnings binaries. The bifurcation between memory strength and logic foundry caution persists.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish on HBM/memory cycle, but logic foundry sector faces near-term earnings risks
semiconductors.key_development: Apple-Broadcom $30B AI chip deal and Micron's EPS acceleration provided stock-specific lifts, offsetting the earlier Samsung miss.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged: MSFT's own-model shift narrowed the OpenAI dependency gap, while AMZN faced bond market concerns and GOOG held steady ahead of earnings. PLTR momentum-chase block locks entry.
big_tech.thesis_status: Divergence narrowing — MSFT's pivot reduces risk, but capex translation still unproven
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's reported shift to its own AI models (Gigazine, July 8) is a positive step toward de-risking the Azure AI revenue concentration.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Direct photonics play — both LITE and COHR received $2B strategic investments from NVDA for AI optics. Moves as an optics basket; watch for earnings/contracts as a read-through on CPO buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE confirms a firm PO from a hyperscaler, or COHR reports above-consensus datacom revenue growth >20% YoY.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier behind SK Hynix and Micron. Samsung's recent earnings miss triggered the semis selloff; its HBM qualification status with NVDA is the key read-through on memory supply diversity and price trends.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if Samsung receives NVDA HBM3E qualification, or after MU earnings confirm demand trajectory.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: Cadence is directly exposed to US EDA software export restrictions to China, which could cap ASML/INTC China revenue. A tightening of rules would disproportionately affect design-tool vendors.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if the US Commerce Department announces new EDA export controls; target a 10-15% pullback from current levels.

active_risks: Iran attacked commercial tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, collapsing the ceasefire. US airstrikes overnight. Tanker traffic has essentially stopped.
Fed June minutes reveal a hawkish split with nine members projecting rate hikes before year-end amid rising inflation expectations (NY Fed survey).
Beijing weighing curbs on overseas access to top AI models, escalating the US-China tech-silicon war (per Reuters, July 7).
Renewed Russian ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv with air defense shortages; Ukraine escalation continues.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil spiked to $74.57, rekindling inflation fears that pushed yields higher and gold lower. Semis bounced sharply on stock-specific news, but energy-sensitive parts of the market fell. Singapore banks are overbought and at risk of a commodity-led pullback.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: The US-Iran ceasefire collapsed after Iran attacked three tankers; the US conducted airstrikes. Trump declared the ceasefire 'over.'

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds at elevated but not crisis oil prices ($70–80). Inflation stalls but doesn't surge. Semis remain bifurcated — memory strong, logic pressured. Fed stays on hold, markets grind sideways.

optimistic.description: Ceasefire restored within weeks, oil retreats to $65. Inflation fears ease, yields fall, and risk appetite returns. Semis rally broadly, with TSMC/ASML earnings acting as positive catalysts.

pessimistic.description: Strait closure tightens further, oil above $100. Energy inflation forces Fed to hike; yields spike, credit tightens. Semis sell off sharply; memory demand holds but valuations compress; Singapore banks NIMs widen but growth stalls.

wildcard.description: Broader escalation draws in renewables/defense stocks (PLTR, BE, LITE) as governments commit to energy independence and secure supply chains, creating a sudden re-rating of those names even as traditional tech sells off.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain defense; no new entries until the oil spike proves transient and VIX settles. The Hormuz escalation has not yet been priced into AI capex assumptions, but prolonged disruption would.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive with select opportunities in memory and foundry binaries
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 4 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the Hormuz oil spike injects fresh inflation risk that could deepen semis caution.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=5) / ACCUMULATE (n=7) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
